hypothesis: If CHROMA's encoder and decoder capacities are increased (from 1B→3B→8B encoders and 2→4→8 decoder layers), then the generated multilayer thin-film designs will produce lower CIE76 ΔE to target colors for both paraphrased prompts and novel optical targets, because larger language encoders and deeper decoders better capture the semantic-to-physical mapping required for accurate structural-color inverse design.
